question: Therapeutic area
fhir_ccda_provenance: n/a — set to "Rare Cancers" at export
note: Fixed value for this registry.

question: Who is reporting this case?
note: Governance decision. A machine-derived case has no reporter until a human attests to it.

question: What disease did the participant receive treatment for?
note: Requires a mapping from ICD-10-CM/SNOMED to the CURE ID controlled disease list. Not one to one; needs a published crosswalk.

question: Sarcoma subtype if not listed
note: ICD-O-3 morphology is the right source and is rarely present in patient-directed FHIR. NLP over the pathology narrative, then human confirmation.

question: Age at diagnosis (banded)
note: Banded to the 18 CURE ID age groups (<1 month through 90+ years). Banding is also the privacy control.

question: Sex assigned at birth
fhir_ccda_provenance: US Core Birth Sex extension; fallback Patient.gender
note: Patient.gender is administrative gender, not sex assigned at birth. Where the extension is absent, ask on the survey rather than infer.

question: Country of treatment
note: Defaults to United States; confirm on survey for participants with care abroad.

question: Race
note: CURE ID uses OMB categories, so the value set aligns. EHR race is often incomplete or defaulted; the survey answer should win.

question: Ethnicity
note: Same precedence rule as race.

question: Predisposing risk factors
note: Agent Orange, 9/11 exposure, environmental toxin exposure, prior radiation or chemotherapy. Effectively never structured in an EHR. This is the clearest argument for keeping the survey arm.

question: Tumor predisposition syndrome
note: A GenomeConnect-style genetic health survey outperforms the EHR here for most participants.

question: Primary tumor location
note: Maps cleanly to the 23-item CURE ID body-site list.

question: Localized / locally advanced / metastatic
note: Structured staging is uncommon in patient-directed pulls. Derive a first pass from secondary-malignancy codes, then confirm.

question: Metastatic site(s)
note: Secondary neoplasm codes are well populated and map directly to the bone/brain/liver/lung/lymph-node list.

question: Year of diagnosis
note: Year only. Do not export full dates.

question: Time from first encounter to diagnosis
note: The EHR estimate is systematically short because it misses care before the connected system. Show the estimate, let the participant correct it.

question: Reason for diagnostic delay
note: Free text. High narrative value; no structured analogue.

question: Signs and symptoms at diagnosis
note: Partially recoverable, but recall from the participant is usually richer than the coded record.

question: Biopsy or resection confirmed
fhir_ccda_provenance: Procedure (SNOMED biopsy) or DiagnosticReport surgical pathology
note: Presence of a surgical pathology report is strong evidence.

question: Expert pathology review
note: Reviewer expertise is not a coded attribute anywhere.

question: Genomic testing performed
note: Presence or absence of the report answers the yes/no reliably.

question: Somatic or germline
note: Usually inferable from the specimen and report header; confirm before export.

question: Molecular abnormality detected
note: Structured where a genomics vendor feed exists; otherwise NLP over the report, then confirmation.

question: Discussed at tumor board
note: Occasionally in notes; not reliably.

question: Previously misdiagnosed
note: Problem-list churn is a useful prompt for the participant, not an answer on its own.

question: Radiation / surgery / systemic therapy
note: The three modality flags are well supported.

question: Best surgical outcome
note: Margin status lives in the pathology narrative. NLP plus confirmation.

question: Systemic drugs administered
note: The core of the submission. Walk every translation child of the coding so an inner RxNorm code is not lost inside an outer NDC.

question: Treatment start
note: Exported as year and month only.

question: Still taking the drug
fhir_ccda_provenance: MedicationStatement.status; last dispense plus days supply
note: A stale record looks identical to an ongoing one. Ask.

question: Treatment end
fhir_ccda_provenance: last dispense + days supply, or effectivePeriod.end
note: Discontinuation is usually implicit. Participant confirmation matters most here.

question: Dose
note: Frequently only a free-text sig. Parse, then show for confirmation.

question: Frequency
note: Same caveat as dose.

question: Route of administration
note: Usually coded.

question: Significant adverse event
fhir_ccda_provenance: Condition / AllergyIntolerance / Observation in temporal proximity
note: Temporal proximity is a signal, not an attribution. Never auto-assert causality; put it to the participant or clinician.

question: Which adverse events
fhir_ccda_provenance: Condition.code; map to the CURE ID adverse-event list
note: PRO-CTCAE items already in this registry give a stronger, participant-sourced answer.

question: Reason stopped
note: Adverse event, progression, participant choice, withdrawal of consent. Intent is not recorded in the EHR.

question: On-label / off-label SOC / novel use
fhir_ccda_provenance: RxNorm ingredient compared with FDA labeled indication (SPL/DailyMed) against Condition.code
note: The pivotal computation of the whole project. Automate the first pass, require human adjudication, and keep the reasoning auditable.

question: In what new way was it used
fhir_ccda_provenance: compare dose, route, schedule, and combination against label
note: New indication versus new dose, schedule, duration, route, combination, or population. Partly computable, partly judgment.

question: Goals of the line of therapy
note: Cure, control, quality of life, symptom relief, prolonged life. Intent is not a coded field.

question: Best outcome on this line
note: RECIST response is almost never structured in patient-directed data. Expect this to come from the survey plus radiology-report NLP.

question: Duration of response
fhir_ccda_provenance: interval between response and progression observations
note: Derivable only where response is structured, which is rare.

question: Outcome ongoing
note: Point-in-time participant report.

question: Other therapy details
note: Free text.

question: Outcome at time of report
note: Loss of life is the one outcome where an external linkage (National Death Index, or a tokenized mortality file) materially improves ascertainment over the portal record.

question: Additional information
note: Free text narrative.

question: Upload images or reports
note: Participant-directed C-CDA already contains documents; offer them for selective attachment rather than asking for a re-upload.
